Iga Review: The Master of Kunai Storm - Empires and Puzzles

Iga brings a unique flavor of destruction to the Ninja family. Instead of immediate devastation, he plants a seed of doom in the form of explosive Kunai Fiends. But in a world of overhealing and rapid cleansing, can this Master of Kunai Storm still make a noise, or is his fuse too long? We dissect his mechanics to see if he's worth the wait.
Iga: The Ticking Time Bomb of the Ninja Tower
Iga, The Master of Kunai Storm: A Ticking Time Bomb Wrapped in Saffron

Iga is a 5-star Legendary Holy hero from the Ninja Tower event, released in late 2024. As a member of the Ninja family, he utilizes the signature Charge mana speed, allowing him to fire at 1x, 2x, or 3x mana. He represents a shift from the direct damage dealers of the past to a more tactical, delayed-gratification playstyle. His entire kit revolves around summoning "Kunai Fiends" that act as ticking time bombs attached to your enemies. He is the guy who whispers "Omae wa mou shindeiru" (You are already dead) three turns before the enemy actually realizes it.

Iga's design is a striking mix of traditional ninja aesthetics and mystical power. He sports a saffron and white outfit with intricate wave patterns on his shin guards and forearm armor, suggesting a connection to fluid, perhaps storm-like movement. His face is obscured by a mask and marked with a glowing rune on his forehead, emphasizing his mystical "Master of Kunai Storm" title. The floating kunai surrounded by golden energy trails give him a dynamic, "caught in the moment of casting" look. It is refreshing to see a ninja who looks like he actually uses magic rather than just hitting things really hard with a katana, although he carries one on his back just in case.
Iga enters the arena with a stat spread that was respectable in 2024 but shows clear signs of aging against the modern titans of 2026.
, Iga still packs a punch, sitting comfortably in the "high attack" bracket, though overshadowed by newer heroes pushing 1350+. His defensive stats, however, are where the cracks show.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Ninja family brings evasive utility, while his passive is a direct counter to the minion meta.
The Ninja family bonus gives a chance to
. While unreliable (startng at 5%), it can occasionally save Iga's fragile life or chip away at an attacker. It is a nice bonus, but not a strategy to rely on.

Enemy minions inherit -50% less Health. This is a massive passive against minion summoners like the Three Kingdoms family or Waterpipe. It effectively turns "meat shields" into "paper shields," making it much easier for your team to cut through minion bloat.

At the start of battle, Iga is immune to new Fiends for 6 turns. This is... situational. It helps against an opposing Iga or Elizabeth, preventing his healing from being stolen, but it doesn't help him survive direct damage, which is his main problem.

Upon release, Iga was terrifying. The prospect of a "guaranteed" 2000+ damage explosion forced players to bring heavy healers or specialized cleansers. He was a menace in Rush wars and a puzzle to solve in standard raids.

The game has evolved towards massive overhealing (Health Boost) and rapid cleansing. Heroes like Hathor, Shaloi, and the Toon healers can easily pump out enough healing to detach the Kunai Fiend before it detonates. Furthermore, newer damage dealers can deal 1500+ damage
. He is no longer a top-tier defense hero because the AI cannot be trusted to time his charges, and human players can easily counter his fiends with healers. However, on

, he remains a potent tool. If you can protect him and time his 2x or 3x charge, he offers a win condition that bypasses high defense stats. He is a "Win More" hero—if you can control the board for 3 turns, you win. If you are struggling to survive, he won't save you.
Note: This review focuses on the base hero as no costume was provided in the context.
Iga faces a dilemma: he needs Attack to scale his Fiend's turn-based damage, but he needs Defense/Health to survive long enough to cast.

2250 damage ignores defense buffs, counterattacks, and damage reduction. It is pure, unadulterated doom.
Halving enemy minion HP is a passive that remains relevant regardless of his stats.
The fiends absorb a massive amount of healing (45-55% Max HP), effectively neutralizing enemy healers for a turn or two.

The damage is back-loaded. In the 3 turns before the explosion, the enemy can kill Iga, heal the fiend away, or win the match.
A strong burst healer (like Vivica or Hathor) can remove the fiend instantly, cancelling the explosion completely.
Very low base defense makes him easy to pick off.
Heroes immune to ailments or fiends completely shut him down.

Iga requires a team that can buy him time. He is the artillery; you need infantry to hold the line.
Whitefang reduces healing efficiency. If the enemy tries to heal off the Kunai Fiend, they will heal for less, failing to reach the absorption cap. The fiend stays, and the explosion happens.
Ludwig protects Iga and feeds him mana to reach x3 charge. Hathor keeps him alive. Once Iga fires x3, the game is effectively over in 3 turns.
Do not pair him with heroes who want to win fast (rush teams) unless you can guarantee his survival. He clashes with "hit and run" styles.

If you pulled Iga, congratulations! You have a nuke. However, do not put him on your defense team. He is too squishy and the AI is too dumb. Use him on offense against teams that lack heavy healers. Prioritize keeping him alive with potions or healers.

Iga is a luxury toy. He is fun, but inconsistent. In the age of 2026, investing Alpha Aethers (LB2) in him is questionable unless you strictly play offensive wars or want a Bloody Battle specialist. His stats are falling behind, and there are more reliable ways to deal damage. He is a "Bench" hero for top 100 play, but a star in mid-tier wars.
The community has had a love-hate relationship with Iga.
"Wtf. So with 3 charges the fiend almost kills everyone every turn? I guess I need to do a 10-pull here."
- A player reacting to his initial (and later nerfed) beta stats.
"This hero single-handedly killed off any minion tactic."
- A succinct analysis of his stat spread from the forums.
"Iga + Whitefang is a good Diaochan counter combo... Whitefang's passive making heals half as effective will prevent removal of Iga's fiends."
- A strategic gem from the community.

Iga is like ordering a gourmet meal at a busy restaurant. It takes a long time to arrive (3 turns!), and there's a chance the waiter drops it (heals/cleanses), but if it actually lands on the table, it is absolutely delicious (2250 damage). He demands patience and protection, traits that are rare in the fast-paced, violent world of Empires & Puzzles. Use him if you like big explosions and have the nerve to watch the fuse burn.
Verdict: Keep him for Bloody Battles and Minion Wars, but don't expect him to anchor your defense.
An in-depth review of Iga, the 5-star Holy Ninja hero from Empires & Puzzles. We analyze his stats, explosive fiend mechanics, and viability in the current meta.
